title: Descriptor-fetch address translation and fabric comparison
result: No concrete source-backed address-translation, IOMMU, descriptor geometry, DMA-mask, or SDMMC0 fabric-clock delta remains that justifies another visible-control kernel build. The next responsible test is a lower-level descriptor-placement/access-path proof.

evidence_schema.vendor_value_at_time_t: iommu@3900000 masters = USB, CSI, ISP, VE_ENC, VE_DEC0, VE_DEC1, DE0, DI, G2D, EINK, DEBUG_MODE; no SDMMC master
evidence_schema.mainline_value_at_time_t: No SDMMC0 iommus property in diagnostic DTS
evidence_schema.interpretation: Do not add an iommus property or chase IOMMU translation for SDMMC0 without new evidence.

evidence_schema.vendor_value_at_time_t: compatible allwinner,sunxi-mmc-v5p3x; clocks osc24m, pll_periph, pll_periph_2, mmc, ahb, mmc_store, mmc_mbus, mmc_msi_lite; reset cell 0x23; req-page-count 2
evidence_schema.mainline_value_at_time_t: diagnostic DTS has ahb, mmc, mmc_store, mmc_mbus, mmc_msi_lite and reset <&ccu 35>
evidence_schema.delta: Parent PLL clock exposure differs by binding style; storage fabric consumers and reset line match the tested diagnostic intent
evidence_schema.interpretation: No DTS expansion is justified for the maintainer-facing slice; prior fabric-clock and MSI-lite tests already failed to move IDMAC.

evidence_schema.address_or_source: vendor v5p3x lines 914-916; BSP descriptor helpers lines 727, 790-794
evidence_schema.vendor_value_at_time_t: des_addr_shift = 2; REG_DLBA and descriptor buffer/next pointers are shifted by 2
evidence_schema.mainline_value_at_time_t: H016 sg_dma 0xfe100000, DLBA 0x3f840000; data DMA 0x101d98000, descriptor buf 0x40766000
evidence_schema.interpretation: Unshifted descriptor/base/data variants remain falsified; do not repeat address-shift tests.
The v5p3x 4 KiB path is already represented; descriptor geometry is closed for now.

evidence_schema.address_or_source: vendor descmem sample lines 2-120; H016 lines 36.685-37.228
evidence_schema.delta: Control and size words match; exact buffer/next addresses differ by allocation
evidence_schema.interpretation: The controller is not rejecting an obviously different descriptor format.

evidence_schema.delta: Both descriptor rings are below 4 GiB and use shifted DLBA, but placement differs
evidence_schema.interpretation: This is the only remaining cheap distinction worth testing directly: replay H016 stamps without the forced 64-bit DMA-mask path to sample the default/pre-64-bit descriptor window.

evidence_schema.vendor_value_at_time_t: descriptor buffer word 0x433b9000, implying data buffer above 4 GiB after undoing shift
evidence_schema.mainline_value_at_time_t: descriptor buffer word 0x40766000, implying data buffer above 4 GiB after undoing shift
evidence_schema.interpretation: The blocker is before data-buffer movement because CBDA remains zero and descriptor OWN is unchanged.

evidence_schema.vendor_value_at_time_t: pdev dma_mask and coherent_dma_mask set to DMA_BIT_MASK(64); descriptor ring allocated by dma_alloc_coherent
evidence_schema.mainline_value_at_time_t: H016 uses the 64-bit DMA-mask diagnostic path and coherent descriptor ring allocation
evidence_schema.delta: No source mismatch in the 64-bit-mask intent
evidence_schema.interpretation: The 64-bit mask itself is falsified as a complete fix, but descriptor-placement replay remains useful because H016 stamp proof only covered the 0xfe100000 allocation.

evidence_schema.vendor_value_at_time_t: init descriptors; reset FIFO, DMA interface, and DMAC; set GCTRL DMA enable; set IDIE for reads; write DMAC fixed-burst plus IDMA-on
evidence_schema.mainline_value_at_time_t: H016 includes vendor-like reset sequence and launches DMAC=0x282
evidence_schema.interpretation: Refetch, fixed-burst, DMAC reset, stale IDST, and ACCESS_BY_AHB variants are already falsified.

closed_without_build: No SDMMC0 IOMMU master or iommus property exists in the vendor DT evidence.
The active descriptor format, descriptor address shift, descriptor segment size, and req-page-count match the vendor v5p3x path or have already been falsified.
The vendor 64-bit DMA-mask setup has already been represented in the diagnostic path and did not fix IDMAC progress.
The storage-fabric clock consumers are present in the diagnostic DTS and prior enable-order/MSI-lite tests did not move the failure.
Visible GCTRL/DMAC/control deltas have been closed by H011-H016.

next_hypothesis.title: Descriptor-stamp replay without forced 64-bit DMA mask
procedure: Branch from the H016 diagnostic commit.

Remove only the A733 64-bit dma_set_mask_and_coherent diagnostic change before descriptor allocation; keep H016 descriptor stamps, CMD49 PIO safety rail, read-only root proof, and forced one-descriptor 8-block CMD18.
Build and boot Cubie3 once from Strix using prompt-controlled UART automation.
Record the new descriptor sg_dma, DLBA, descriptor words, checksum/stamps, CHDA, CBDA, IDST, CBCR, and BBCR before and after the stall.
Restore Cubie3 to vendor 5.15.147-21-a733 and update the queue/docs.
next_hypothesis.interpretation: If the descriptor ring remains unchanged and CHDA=DLBA/CBDA=0 at the default lower placement too, the descriptor-fetch blocker is independent of the H016 0xfe100000 descriptor window. If IDMAC advances, the forced 64-bit allocation/high-low descriptor placement becomes the primary suspect.
